heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: On 09 November 2025, the rain lasted for 144.8 hours, which is the longest time recorded by our database.  47.89mm accumulated and it fell at 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: A storm is passing through the area.  This marks the first storm since 21 December 2025.

top_10_dry.status: We are in the midst of one of the longest dry periods recorded.  This dry period is ranked 7th since records began in 23 September 2024.  The current stretch started on 23 December 2025 and has lasted 9 days.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C), set on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwaves right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The most recent tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and this has only occurred 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (197°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 8.3°C), outside temperature (down 5.1°C), and feels like temperature (down 4.3°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 285 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 9.0°C), outside temperature (up 9.1°C), cloud levels (up 321 m), and inside humidity (up 7%) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 284.7m lower than yesterday, 321.3m lower than the past week, 33.7m higher than the past month.

Yesterday’s cloud levels showed a low of 828m and a peak of 2280m, averaging 996.2m; the week and month recorded warmer averages.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate mild and comfortable temperatures. Outside; bitterly cold temperatures are expected. Also, dry air conditions likely prevail and very little to no wind will be present. Showers, occasional overcast conditions, and bright intervals likely. High pressure suggests settled weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-5.8°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
